topic: Areas of Inquiry: Music Technology in the Electronic and Digital Age
subject: music

items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.

items.q: How many crotchets are in a semibreve?
items.why: Students confuse the relative durations. A semibreve (whole note) = 2 minims = 4 crotchets = 8 quavers. Each level halves in length.

items.correct: Rapid alternation between the written note and the note a step above.
items.why: Students confuse trill (rapid alternation between two notes) with vibrato (pitch oscillation around one note). Trills add ornamentation; vibrato adds expressive width to a sustained pitch.
It's Leon Theremin. “Robert Moog” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Whose 1956 Gesang der Junglinge mixed boy's voice with electronics?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Stockhausen. “Berio” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was Ableton Live first released?
items.why: The real figure is 2001 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
